sources.title: 1989 - War Requiem - Directed by Derek Jarman
sources.description:
Wilfred Owen, a renowned English poet, volunteers to serve on the front lines during World War I. Stationed in the trenches of Flanders, what he witnesses horrifies him and drives him to write a long poem depicting his experience with biting bitterness and haunting words. He would die on November 4, 1918 — just one week before the armistice that ended the war.

⭐️ STARRING
Nathaniel Parker as Wilfred Owen
Tilda Swinton as The Nurse
Laurence Olivier as The Old Soldier
Patricia Hayes as Mother
Sean Bean as The German Soldier

🎬 CREW
Written & Directed by Derek Jarman 
Music by Benjamin Britten (War Requiem)
Cinematography by Richard Greatrex

WHY WATCH?
Sir Laurence Olivier's final bow – a moving performance that echoes through time
Unique sensory experience: Britten's choral masterpiece fused with Jarman's haunting visuals
Cult cinema gold – an avant-garde war film that shatters conventions like a grenade in a gallery!

🔔 SUBSCRIBE @CultCinemaClassics
War • Drama • Experimental

metadata.Plot: A movie with no spoken dialogue, it is set against the music and lyrics of Benjamin Britten's "War Requiem" which includes poetry by World War I soldier Wilfred Owen reflecting the horrors of war. There is no linear story or dialogue. It's imagery reflects Owen's story, that of other soldiers, and a nurse during World War I. It also includes actual footage of contemporary wars, including World War II, Vietnam, and Angola.
metadata.Actors: Nathaniel Parker, Tilda Swinton, Laurence Olivier
